content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660         322-5660           322-5001        322-5441              322-6444

February 22, 1982


Mr. James Curlett
Holtzman, Wise & Shepard
755 Page Mill Road
Palo Alto, CA  94304

Dear Mr. Curlett:

On December 24, 1981, I sent you a letter stating that
the Political Reform Act does not prohibit corporations from
contributing to state and local ballot measures.    You now
ask whether the term "corporation," as used in my letter,
refers to both domestic and foreign corporations.  It does.

I want to again recommend that you consult the restrictions of the Federal Election Campaign Act which prohibits
contributions from foreign nationals in connection with any
local, state or federal election for political office.  (2
USC §441e.)  (See p.17 of the enclosed 1982 Informational
Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions.)  Also, you may
want to contact the U.S. Attorney's Office, any appropriate
local entities and the California Attorney General's Office
regarding restrictions which may exist under laws other than
the Political Reform Act.

Very truly yours,


Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:km
Enclosure


1/  Corporations which make contributions must comply
with any applicable disclosure requirements under the Act.

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660               322-5901            322-6441              322-6444

December 24, 1981

Mr. James Curlett
Holtzman, Wise and Shepard
755 Page Mill Road
Palo Alto, CA 94304

Dear Mr. Curlett:

Thank you for your telephone call requesting advice from
this office. Your question was whether the Political Reform Act
contains any prohibitions against corporations contributing to
state and local candidates and ballot measures.

The Political Reform Act does not prohibit such contributions,
however, corporations making contributions must comply with any
applicable disclosure requirements under the Act. During our
conversation you expressed familiarity with these disclosure
provisions.

I had hoped to be able to send you a copy of a memorandum
currently being prepared on 1982 filing requirements for General
Purpose Committees, but it was not completed in time to be sent
to you. I have enclosed a 1981 "Informational Manual on Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act." Please note
that the filing dates listed in the manual will be different in
1982 and that the correct dates may be obtained by contacting
Jeanne Pritchard at 916/322-5662.

As we discussed, federal and local limitations on corporate
contributions should be considered by you before the corporation
in question makes any contributions. I recommend that you contact
the U.S. Attorney's Office, the Federal Election Commission and
any appropriate local entities for more information. In addition,
you may want to contact the California Attorney General's Office
regarding restrictions that may exist under laws other than the
Political Reform Act.

If you have additional questions, please feel free to contact
me at 916/322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:km
Enclosure
